types.name: Int
types.description: The `Int` scalar type represents non-fractional signed whole numeric values. Int can represent values between -(2^31) and 2^31 - 1.

types.name: String
types.description: The `String` scalar type represents textual data, represented as UTF-8 character sequences. The String type is most often used by GraphQL to represent free-form human-readable text.

types.name: Image
types.description: Represents a media image asset associated with videos, audios, series, genres, or campaigns, including metadata such as URL, source category, and color palette.

fields.name: palette
fields.description: The color palette of the image. A JSON object in this schema: { light: { base, accent, textPrimary, textSecondary, backgroundGradient: { "0", "25", "50", "75", "100" } }, dark: { base, accent, textPrimary, textSecondary, backgroundGradient: { "0", "25", "50", "75", "100" } } }

types.name: Serie
types.description: TV or video series this video is a member of. At Bibel TV also called "Sendereihen".

fields.name: custom
fields.deprecationReason: Kept for backward compatibility. Use dedicated typed fields instead.

fields.name: seasons
fields.description: Which seasons are available to watch

fields.name: isFictional
fields.description: Differentiate if this Serie is fictional or not

fields.name: crew
fields.description: People who helped to create the movie

types.name: Video
types.description:
Our "core-entity": Data model for videos; following (as far as possible) IPTC Video Metadata Hub
Recommendation 1.3 https://iptc.org/standards/video-metadata-hub/recommendation/

fields.name: crn
fields.description: CRN (Content Reference Number) der Sendung (Unique identifier)

fields.name: title
fields.description: Title of the video, should be a shorthand reference. Sendungstitel (bei Episoden Titel der Episode). Ggf. Länge gem. VAUNET beachten?

fields.name: slug
fields.description: The slug is made by getting the CRN + title. Replace the spaces with dash (-). Also replace german special (ä, ö, ü, ß)characters with common letters (ae, o, u, ss). Remove all non alphanumeric characters, like dots or parenthesis.

fields.name: description
fields.description:
Textual description of the content of the video.
Kurzbeschreibung (darf bei Brightcove nur 248 Zeichen haben) -> Müssen wir auf Long Description. Inhaltsbeschreibung kurz. In MF Inhaltsfeld "EPG-Kurztext". VAUNET: max. 240 Zeichen (mit Leerzeichen)

fields.name: parentCrn
fields.description: CRN des Eltern-Elements (bspw. Serien CRN bei Episode oder CRN der entsprechenden Sendung bei Trailern

fields.name: keywords
fields.description: Tags (in der XML mit "#" als Trennzeichen; bei Brightcove mit "," als Trennzeichen

fields.name: duration
fields.description: in seconds

fields.name: isoDuration
fields.description: The length of the video. The property value is an ISO 8601 duration. For example, for a video that is at least one minute long and less than one hour long, the duration is in the format PT#M#S, in which the letters PT indicate that the value specifies a period of time, and the letters M and S refer to length in minutes and seconds, respectively. The # characters preceding the M and S letters are both integers that specify the number of minutes (or seconds) of the video. For example, a value of PT15M33S indicates that the video is 15 minutes and 33 seconds long.

fields.name: productionCountry
fields.description: Produktionland bzw -länder. In MF bis zu 3 hinterlegbar. Abkürzung nach ISO-3166 Alpha-2. Aus VAUNET: Angabe des Produktionsjahres betrifft bei (Kino-)Filmen den Kinostart der Originalfassung, bei TV-Produktionen das Jahr der Erstausstrahlung

fields.name: descriptionLong
fields.description: Inhaltsbeschreibung lang. In MF Inhaltsfeld "EPG"

fields.name: ageRecommendation
fields.description: Bibel TV interne Altersempfehlung. Aus MF.

fields.name: contentRating
fields.description: Für die Bibel Tv eigenen Inhaltskategorien aus MF

fields.name: language
fields.description: In MF sind bis zu zwei Sprachen möglich

fields.name: personsShown
fields.description: Person(s) shown by the video (cast)
fields.deprecationReason: Deprecated, please use the crew field instead

fields.name: custom
fields.deprecationReason: Kept for backward compatibility. Use the dedicated typed fields (e.g. partnerText) instead.

fields.name: partnerText
fields.description: Partner text shown on the video page. A JSON object in this schema: { text: string, btnText: string, link: string }.

fields.name: introTime
fields.description: The time in seconds where the intro starts and ends. First value is the start, second value is the end.

fields.name: isMediaLibrary
fields.description: Whether or not this entry should be shown in the Mediathek

fields.name: crew
fields.description: People who helped to create the movie

fields.name: active
fields.description: if the video is published or not

fields.name: ovpId
fields.description: The same kalturaId in v1

fields.name: ovpName
fields.description: Aus welcher OVP kommen die Daten?

fields.name: videoUrls
fields.deprecationReason: This field will be removed as soon all clients are updated

types.name: CuePoint
types.description: For video cards and suggestions

types.name: Transcript
types.description: Transcript for a video

types.name: Float
types.description: The `Float` scalar type represents signed double-precision fractional values as specified by [IEEE 754](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IEEE_floating_point).

fields.name: src
fields.description: video file URL

fields.name: type
fields.description: video type

fields.description:
Textual description of the content of the audio.
Kurzbeschreibung (darf bei Brightcove nur 248 Zeichen haben) -> Müssen wir auf Long Description. Inhaltsbeschreibung kurz. In MF Inhaltsfeld "EPG-Kurztext". VAUNET: max. 240 Zeichen (mit Leerzeichen)

fields.name: isoDuration
fields.description: The length of the audio. The property value is an ISO 8601 duration. For example, for a audio that is at least one minute long and less than one hour long, the duration is in the format PT#M#S, in which the letters PT indicate that the value specifies a period of time, and the letters M and S refer to length in minutes and seconds, respectively. The # characters preceding the M and S letters are both integers that specify the number of minutes (or seconds) of the audio. For example, a value of PT15M33S indicates that the audio is 15 minutes and 33 seconds long.

types.name: Playlist
types.description: Playliste als redaktionelle oder automatische Zusammenstellung von Videos.

fields.name: color
fields.description: Color value used by the client (e.g. the Kids app).

types.name: PlaylistCollection
types.description: Eine Sammlung aus verschiedenen Playlisten. Insbesondere, um so unterschiedliche Applikationen zu befüllen.

types.name: IpLookup
types.description: Geo IP API

types.name: VerseItem
types.description: From bibleVerses entry string. Convert time codes to seconds

fields.name: time
fields.description: convert 1,22 to 82.

fields.name: pageUrl
fields.description: Full URL of the landing/watch page for this livestream. For internal streams this is the Bibel TV page URL; for externally hosted streams (e.g. echtjetzt.tv) it is the external domain URL. Nullable — not all streams have a dedicated page. Editable via the CMS.

types.name: DateTime
types.description: A date-time string at UTC, such as 2007-12-03T10:15:30Z, compliant with the `date-time` format outlined in section 5.6 of the RFC 3339 profile of the ISO 8601 standard for representation of dates and times using the Gregorian calendar.

fields.name: nextEpisodeOf
fields.description: Next video suggested after the given video CRN. Falls through serie, playlists, and sub-genres.

args.name: crn
args.description: The CRN of the current video you want to know the next episode.

types.name: __Schema
types.description: A GraphQL Schema defines the capabilities of a GraphQL server. It exposes all available types and directives on the server, as well as the entry points for query, mutation, and subscription operations.

fields.name: types
fields.description: A list of all types supported by this server.

fields.name: queryType
fields.description: The type that query operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: mutationType
fields.description: If this server supports mutation, the type that mutation operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: subscriptionType
fields.description: If this server support subscription, the type that subscription operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: directives
fields.description: A list of all directives supported by this server.

types.name: __Type
types.description:
The fundamental unit of any GraphQL Schema is the type. There are many kinds of types in GraphQL as represented by the `__TypeKind` enum.

Depending on the kind of a type, certain fields describe information about that type. Scalar types provide no information beyond a name, description and optional `specifiedByURL`, while Enum types provide their values. Object and Interface types provide the fields they describe. Abstract types, Union and Interface, provide the Object types possible at runtime. List and NonNull types compose other types.

types.name: __TypeKind
types.description: An enum describing what kind of type a given `__Type` is.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a scalar.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an object. `fields` and `interfaces` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an interface. `fields`, `interfaces`, and `possibleTypes` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a union. `possibleTypes` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an enum. `enumValues` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an input object. `inputFields` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: LIST
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a list. `ofType` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: NON_NULL
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a non-null. `ofType` is a valid field.

types.name: __Field
types.description: Object and Interface types are described by a list of Fields, each of which has a name, potentially a list of arguments, and a return type.

types.name: __InputValue
types.description: Arguments provided to Fields or Directives and the input fields of an InputObject are represented as Input Values which describe their type and optionally a default value.

fields.name: defaultValue
fields.description: A GraphQL-formatted string representing the default value for this input value.

types.name: __EnumValue
types.description: One possible value for a given Enum. Enum values are unique values, not a placeholder for a string or numeric value. However an Enum value is returned in a JSON response as a string.

types.name: __Directive
types.description:
A Directive provides a way to describe alternate runtime execution and type validation behavior in a GraphQL document.

In some cases, you need to provide options to alter GraphQL's execution behavior in ways field arguments will not suffice, such as conditionally including or skipping a field. Directives provide this by describing additional information to the executor.

types.name: __DirectiveLocation
types.description: A Directive can be adjacent to many parts of the GraphQL language, a __DirectiveLocation describes one such possible adjacencies.

enumValues.name: QUERY
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a query operation.

enumValues.name: MUTATION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a mutation operation.

enumValues.name: SUBSCRIPTION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a subscription operation.

enumValues.name: FIELD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment definition.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_SPREAD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment spread.

enumValues.name: INLINE_FRAGMENT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an inline fragment.

enumValues.name: VARIABLE_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a variable definition.

enumValues.name: SCHEMA
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a schema definition.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a scalar definition.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an object type definition.

enumValues.name: FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field definition.

enumValues.name: ARGUMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an argument definition.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an interface definition.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a union definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM_VALUE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum value definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object type definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object field definition.

args.name: reason
args.description: Explains why this element was deprecated, usually also including a suggestion for how to access supported similar data. Formatted using the Markdown syntax, as specified by [CommonMark](https://commonmark.org/).

args.name: url
args.description: The URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.
